blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Did he know. At the end — do you think he knew something was wrong?

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: I don't think so. I think he just — I think he went to sleep, Mrs. Calloway. I think it was quiet.
He didn't know if that was true. He said it anyway. Some lies are the only kind thing left.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: He called me Thursday. The day before you all went down. He called to check on my back — I've been having trouble with my back and he always remembered to ask about it. He talked to me for twenty minutes about nothing. The weather, a restaurant he'd tried, some show he was watching. Twenty minutes.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: It does, doesn't it. It sounds exactly like him.
Her voice broke on the last word. She didn't try to hold it. Dean pressed his fist against his mouth and looked at the water and waited.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: He was so proud of all of you. He talked about this trip. He was looking forward to it so much.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: We were all looking forward to it.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: I know. I know you were.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Is there anything — did he seem alright to you? This weekend? Was there anything different about him?
Dean thought about Marcus on the beach. Zoning out. The look on his face that wasn't entirely there. He thought about telling her.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: He seemed a little tired. That's all. He made us all breakfast Saturday morning. He was laughing. He was — he was himself.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Good. That's good. I'm glad he was laughing.
Dean couldn't speak for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Joyce
blocks.content: Don't apologize. Don't you dare apologize. You called me. Do you know how much it means that you called me.
